criteria.label: Recognize which flavor (opposite-ends, fast/slow, dual-sequence) of two-pointer applies to a problem.
criteria.example: Pair-sum on sorted: opposite ends. In-place dedup: fast/slow. Merge sorted: dual cursors.

criteria.label: Default to half-open intervals `[l, r)` and update the window state incrementally.
criteria.example: `run += arr[r]` on expand; `run -= arr[l]; l += 1` on shrink.

criteria.label: Identify whether the validity predicate is monotonic / restorable before reaching for variable-size sliding window.
criteria.example: Sum ≤ K (monotonic). Distinct ≤ K (monotonic). Median ≥ K (NOT monotonic — use DP/heaps).

criteria.label: Shrink with `while`, not `if`, to restore validity in one logical step.
Use a counter / hash map inside the window for O(1) membership/count operations.
criteria.example: `from collections import Counter; c = Counter(); c[arr[r]] += 1`

criteria.label: Implement rolling max / min with a monotonic deque in amortized O(n).
criteria.example: Keep `deque` of indices with values strictly decreasing; `dq[0]` is the current window max.
